message:
feat(replay): tick 165 handle_control_with_cx closes cancellation hole in Pause branch (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Sixteenth post-storage tick. Fixes a latent cancellation hole that
had survived the initial replay.rs cx-first migration.

Problem: the legacy `handle_control` has a Pause branch that spins
on `control_rx.changed(&watch_cx)` where
`watch_cx = crate::cx::for_request()` — a fresh orphan cx. That
means even if the caller of `play_with_cx` cancels their cx, the
paused player will block forever inside the watch-channel wait
until an external Play/Stop control signal wakes it. The outer
loop's `cx.checkpoint()` runs only between frames, not during a
Pause wait.

New sibling:
- handle_control_with_cx — identical match structure, but the
  Pause branch:
  (a) runs `cx.checkpoint()` on every loop iteration,
  (b) passes the caller's cx into `control_rx.changed(cx)` so
      the watch-channel wait itself is interruptible by cx
      cancellation, not just by control-channel closure.

Call-site migrations in `play_with_cx` (L668):
- Line 689 (first `handle_control` call inside the per-frame
  check) → `handle_control_with_cx(cx, ctrl, ...)`
- Line 713 (second `handle_control` call after the inter-frame
  sleep) → `handle_control_with_cx(cx, ctrl, ...)`

Legacy `play` (L596) + `handle_control` preserved unchanged; its
two call sites (L606 / L626) still route through the ambient
handler.

This plugs the last behavior gap between `play` and `play_with_cx`
— a paused player under a cx-first caller now exits within one
cancel-signal tick (one changed() wait wake-up) instead of
requiring external control input.

Verified via fork bypass: 203/203 replay:: tests pass, including
the existing `play_simple_with_cx_matches_legacy` byte-parity
gate.

replay.rs cx-first coverage: 3/3 public async fns (play,
play_simple, handle_control).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
